articles.headline: Air Canada Flight Attendant Thrown 100 Meters In Jump Seat During LaGuardia Crash

articles.headline: Why Newer Aircraft Aren’t Always Better
articles.blurb: The newest jets promise efficiency and luxury, but hidden trade-offs lurk beneath the shiny exterior.
articles.takeaway: Legacy aircraft often outperform newer models in reliability and total cost of ownership despite advanced technology, creating a paradox that challenges fleet modernization assumptions.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Integrated operational optimization across airlines, airports, and air traffic control can simultaneously increase capacity and reduce environmental impact as demand continues rising.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Remembering the Green Ramp Disaster

articles.takeaway: The 1994 Green Ramp disaster killed 24 paratroopers and injured 83 others, making it the deadliest U.S. military aviation accident on the ground and highlighting critical gaps in airspace management at military bases.
